cards.title: HuggingFace Blog: Profiling Attention in PyTorch — From Naive to Flash-Style Kernels
cards.why_it_matters: If you optimize transformer inference or training, understanding how each attention primitive maps to GPU kernels is directly actionable for your perf work.
cards.summary: Part 3 of HuggingFace's PyTorch profiling series walks through naive causal attention (matmul → scale → masked_fill → softmax → matmul), shows how to read CPU and GPU profiler traces, explains an unexpected memory-copy kernel from out-of-place ops, then contrasts in-place ops, scaled dot-product attention (SDPA), and fused kernels. Scripts run on an A100-SXM4-80GB.

cards.title: Systematic Theory of Hockey Stick f-Divergences Extended to von Neumann Algebras
cards.why_it_matters: For ML researchers working on quantum information-theoretic foundations of learning or hypothesis testing, this unifies divergence measures relevant to quantum channel analysis.
cards.summary: This paper extends hockey stick f-divergences — built on quantum hockey stick divergences — to non-normalized states, more general integral representations with an additional real parameter, and general von Neumann algebras. Key results: representation via Neyman-Pearson error probabilities, extension of Jenčová's channel-reversibility detection criterion, and a proof that regularized hockey stick Rényi α-divergences coincide with Petz-type divergences for α∈(0,1) and sandwiched divergences for α>1.
